subject.name: Object Oriented Programming and Methodology
subject.title: AD/AL/CD/CY/IS-305 (GS) – Object Oriented Programming and Methodology

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Describe the various control statement with example.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Discuss and compare run time and compile time polymorphism.

subQuestions.text: How inherited method is different from redefined method?

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Design a class called as FATHER and MOTHER as base classes and class CHILD which is derived from both FATHER and MOTHER. Write a C++ program to display the qualities of CHILD due to inheritance.

subQuestions.text: What are the various stream classes and stream error? Explain them in brief. What are input and output streams? Give examples.

subQuestions.text: Explain the concept of association and aggregation in object-oriented approach. What is Abstract class? Why Abstract classes are used?

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: How does Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) differ from procedural programming, and what are the key features of the object-oriented paradigm?

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Discuss the merits and demerits of using object-oriented methodology in software development, compared to other programming paradigms.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Describe the concept of exception handling in object-oriented programming, including the mechanisms used to handle runtime errors and exceptions gracefully.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Describe the process of Input/Output (I/O) processing in object-oriented programming, including the handling of data types, type conversion and control statements.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Discuss the concepts of encapsulation and data abstraction in object-oriented programming and explain how they enable modular and reusable code design.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain the concept of objects in object-oriented programming, including their state, behavior and identity and how they are represented in code.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Discuss attributes and services in the context of object-oriented programming, including the role of access modifier and static members in defining class behavior.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain the concept of instances in object-oriented programming and how they represent individual objects created from a class template?

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Describe the process of message passing in object-oriented programming and its role in enabling communication between objects.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Discuss the construction and destruction of objects in object-oriented programming, including the use of constructors and destructors to manage object lifecycle.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Discuss association and aggregation as relationship types in object-oriented programming and provide examples of each.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Describe the concept of interfaces and abstract classes in object-oriented programming, including their role in defining common behavior and enforcing contracts.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Discuss virtual functions, friend functions and static functions in object-oriented programming, including their usage and implications for class design.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain how strings are handled in object-oriented programming, including common operations and best practices for string manipulation.

Introduce the concepts of multi-threading and data collections in object-oriented programming and discuss their applications in building concurrent and data-intensive systems. Additionally, provide a case study, such as an ATM or library management system, to illustrate the practical application of object-oriented thinking and programming principles.

subQuestions.text: Define the object model. What are the core elements of an object model in OOP?

subQuestions.text: What are the main features of the object-oriented paradigm and how do they contribute to its advantages and disadvantages?

subQuestions.text: How are objects constructed and destroyed in OOP and what are the key concepts involved in this process?

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Explain the concept of objects with respect to their state, behaviour and identity.

What is a friend function in C++? Why and when is it used? What are the limitations of friend functions in terms of encapsulation and security?

subQuestions.text: How do you handle exceptions in a program and what are some best practices for exception handling?
